hud-fidelity.rationale: From a feasibility lens the design passes both terminal tests in spirit. Sparkline test: '▁▂▂▃▅▇▇▆' is U+2581–U+2588 (East Asian Width = Neutral, guaranteed single-cell) — that subset is safe. Spellcheck test: gutter '●' col 0 and end-of-row '✗' both ride the line of work. ≤3 primitives per row holds on continuation rows (gutter + verb + desc) but the freshest row carries gutter + verb + bold-weight + badge = 4 — at the brief's ceiling. Two ink decisions are also overstated: 'freshest' is double-encoded (gutter '●' AND Tier-1 bold on the description); the sparkline label '7/min' restates what the shape shows. The bigger feasibility hit on this criterion is that a HUD primitive only counts as HUD if it actually renders — and the sparkline's data wiring (window, buckets, refresh, scaling) is left in Open Questions 1/2/7, so what reaches the screen at build-time is not yet specified.

four-questions.rationale: All four answers are gestured at: liveness from sparkline existence, pattern from its shape, exception from the row-end '✗', latest from the column-0 '●'. Penalties from feasibility: (1) Pattern via sparkline conflates 'stuck' (recent-but-unchanging agent) with 'idle' (no events) — both render as '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁' and Open Q7 explicitly leaves the empty-state alphabet undefined. (2) Exception is encoded as a single end-of-row glyph that collides with the existing splitOutcome+pre-truncate path (Open Q8 unresolved); on narrow widths the badge column is the first thing the truncator eats unless explicitly reserved, so 'did anything go wrong' may vanish at 30 cols on long descriptions. (3) Latest is over-encoded (gutter '●' + Tier-1 bold) — a single instrument would be cleaner. The four answers exist on paper; only one (latest) is uncontested in the build.

terminal-feasibility.rationale: The design is implementable in principle but the spec has multiple 'where would I get stuck tomorrow' holes. (1) SPARKLINE CONTRACT IS ENTIRELY UNSPECIFIED. Open Q1/2/7/10 leave six load-bearing decisions in the air: data source (count of metadata.logs[] entries? debounced 'changed-signal' rate the runtime emits? events/sec?), bucket count and width (8 cells × what window?), refresh policy (re-bucket on log arrival? 1Hz tick? requestAnimationFrame?), y-axis rule (auto-rescale to local max — which makes a single entry render as full block — or fixed max with saturation?), empty-state alphabet (suppress, flat '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁', or replaced with phrase?), and unfocus behaviour (slide via tier? severity-bypass like spinner glyphs? identity-bypass?). The runtime/log producer is OUT OF SCOPE per the brief, so the sparkline must be derived purely from the existing logs[] stream as it stands — and the spec doesn't say how. (2) CODEPOINT BUDGET BLOWN OR HAND-WAVED. The brief allows ≤6 new nerd-font codepoints with rationale per glyph. Candidate uses '●' (U+25CF, geometric, East Asian Width Ambiguous — risks double-cell on some terminal+font combos), '✓' (U+2713, Ambiguous), '✗' (U+2717, Ambiguous), and floats '⚠' (U+26A0, East Asian Width WIDE — will render 2 cells in many terminals, breaks column 0 alignment) as marks without committing them to vocab.ts. None of those four are nerd-font glyphs and none are in the existing vocab. The five verb glyphs in Open Q9 (eye/book-open, format-list-bulleted, magnify, pencil, play-circle) are MDI codepoints and would be safe single-cell, but they are not committed — they are listed as candidates. Tally: potentially 5 verb + 1 gutter + 2–3 badges = 8–9 new glyphs against a 6-glyph budget. (3) VERB-GLYPH PARSER NOT SOURCED. The mockup maps 'Reading build.ts' → eye glyph, but logs[] entries are { message, source, ts, tone } with no verb tag — the renderer would have to regex-classify message strings client-side, and the spec doesn't define that classifier or its fallback. The brief forbids modifying the log producer to add a verb field. (4) MULTI-SOURCE BURST × EYEBROW × CAP=5. With cap of 5–7 visible rows, every source change costs a whole row to a 7-char eyebrow plus the air row already at the top; an interleaving 'pi'/'cc' burst at 1–2 entries per source eats 30–40% of the vertical budget. Open Q5 names the trade-off but leaves it unresolved. (5) SYSTEM-TAGGED SOURCE ('[bell]') has no eyebrow rule — currently rendered with tone color inline; whether '[bell]' becomes an eyebrow row, an inline tag, or a severity-glyph in column 1 is undefined. (6) OUTCOME × TRUNCATION. The current renderer reserves outcome-text width before pre-truncating description; the spec proposes a row-end badge but doesn't say how the badge column interacts with truncateText() — is the last cell reserved for badge-or-blank, or is the badge appended after pre-truncation runs? (7) UNFOCUS STATE for sparkline glyphs and verb glyphs is in Open Q10 — the tier system says severity colours and identity glyphs bypass the slide; a sparkline glyph is text, a verb glyph is structural — the spec must pick. (8) NO ASCII MOCKS for empty / multi-source / error-heavy / 30-col states despite the brief explicitly requesting them. Implementable, but a build attempt tomorrow stalls inside any one of (1)–(8).

asi_candidate.summary: Pin down the sparkline contract — its data source, bucket geometry, refresh rate, y-axis rule, unfocus behaviour, and empty-state alphabet — so the load-bearing HUD primitive is buildable from the existing logs[] stream alone.
asi_candidate.actionable_change: In §Proposed direction, replace Open Questions 1, 2, 7, and the sparkline half of 10 with a resolved §Sparkline contract subsection that commits to all six dimensions: (a) DATA SOURCE — bucket count of metadata.logs[] entries by ts, derived purely client-side (the log producer is out-of-scope per the brief); (b) GEOMETRY — 8 cells × 8s/bucket = 64s window, oldest cell on the left, freshest on the right; (c) REFRESH — re-bucket on every logs[] update plus a 1Hz tick to slide the window when no new entries arrive (no rAF, no per-frame work); (d) Y-AXIS — auto-rescale to max(localMax, 1) so a single entry renders as one block at the top tick rather than a full column, eliminating the 'one event = saturated' artefact; (e) GLYPH ALPHABET — '▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█' (U+2581–U+2588, East Asian Width Neutral, no codepoint budget cost); (f) UNFOCUS — sparkline cells slide via Tier 3 → Tier 4 colour (treated as text, NOT severity-bypass; the rate is not a severity), and the optional '7/min' label drops to Tier 4 muted; (g) EMPTY STATE — when logs[] is empty, suppress the sparkline row entirely (the existing '(no recent activity)' Tier-4 line carries the no-signal state cleanly); when logs[] is non-empty but the 64s window is empty (stale/stuck agent), render '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁' in Tier 4 muted with the label '0/min' so 'idle' and 'stuck' read as the same low-energy shape but distinct from 'empty'. Add one ASCII mock per state (empty / single-source / multi-source / error-heavy / 30-col) showing the sparkline row with the resolved contract applied.
asi_candidate.why_this_lens: From the implementation-realist lens, the question 'if I tried to build this tomorrow, where would I get stuck?' has one dominant answer for this candidate: the sparkline. It is the design's novel HUD primitive (and load-bearing for two of the four watcher questions — liveness, pattern), but the spec hand-waves data source, refresh rate, scaling, and unfocus — which the brief explicitly penalises ('Penalise designs that hand-wave a primitive — sparkline goes here — without specifying its data source, refresh rate, glyph alphabet, and unfocus behaviour'). The other gaps (codepoint budget, verb-glyph parser, eyebrow row-cost) are fixable inside known patterns; the sparkline is the only primitive that is genuinely new and genuinely undefined. Resolving it converts the design's biggest perceptible-by-glance bet from a 'goes here' label into a function the renderer can actually call against the existing logs[] stream, and it does so without leaving the search space — no new producer fields, no new vocab budget, no new OpenTUI capability.
asi_candidate.scope: single-section edit to docs/simmer/activity-zone/iteration-1-candidate.md: insert a §Sparkline contract subsection inside §Proposed direction with the six commitments above; remove items 1, 2, 7, and the sparkline half of 10 from §Open design questions; add five small ASCII mocks (empty / single-source / multi-source / error-heavy / 30-col) showing the resolved sparkline row.

notes_for_phase_2: Adjacent feasibility holes I am NOT folding into the ASI but want flagged in synthesis: (a) the codepoint budget is over-quota when '●', '✓', '✗' are counted as new vocab — a follow-up iteration should commit codepoints (e.g. nf-md-circle-medium U+F09DE for the gutter, nf-md-check U+F012C / nf-md-close U+F0156 for badges) and explicitly retire any East Asian Width Wide glyph like '⚠' U+26A0; (b) the verb-glyph parser (message → verb) needs a client-side classifier spec since the producer is out-of-scope; (c) outcome-badge × pre-truncation needs a reserved-column rule.

If another panelist proposes dropping the sparkline entirely (e.g. replacing it with a numeric rate or a gutter-mass exception count), my ASI dies cleanly — either the sparkline gets a contract or it goes; both resolve the hand-wave penalty.

The information-designer lens already proposed pinning down the verb-glyph column's small-multiples discipline. That ASI and mine are complementary and non-conflicting: theirs closes column 1, mine closes the sparkline row; together they convert the two novel HUD instruments from sketches into specs.
